items.docs:
Adds a network disk to be used to create network volume offers, or adds machines to an existing network disk.
CLI Usage: vast add network_disk <machine_id>... <mount_point> [options]

Lists a network disk for rent as network volumes, or updates an existing listing with a new price/size/end date/discount
CLI Usage: vast list network-volume <disk_id> [options]

Creates a network volume from an offer
CLI Usage: vast create network-volume <offer_id>

Search for available GPU machine offers with advanced filtering and sorting.
CLI Usage: ``` vast search network-volumes --order=storage_cost 'reliability > 0.99 geolocation in [US, CA]'```

Unlists a network volume for rent
CLI Usage: vast unlist network-volume <offer_id>

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to attach the SSH key to

items.docs:
Attaches an SSH key to the specified instance, allowing SSH access using the provided key.

CLI Usage: vast attach instance_id ssh_key
Initiate a remote copy operation to transfer data from one instance to another or between an instance and the local machine.
Cancel a remote copy operation specified by the destination ID (dst_id).
Starts a cloud copy operation by sending a command to the remote server. The operation can transfer data between an instance and a cloud service.

Cancels an in-progress remote sync operation identified by the destination instance ID.
This operation cannot be resumed once canceled and must be restarted if needed.

params.name: id
params.description: Instance ID

items.docs:
Change the current bid price of an instance to a specified price.
CLI Usage: vastai change bid id [--price PRICE]

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the offer to accept

items.docs:
Creates a new instance by accepting an "ask" contract from a provider. This is the main endpoint for launching new instances on Vast.ai.
CLI Usage: vast create instance <offer_id> [options]

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the instance to retrieve.

items.docs:
Retrieves the details of a specific instance for the authenticated user.
This endpoint returns detailed information including SSH connection parameters, instance state, resource utilization, template data, and pricing details.

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to modify

items.docs:
Manage instance state and labels. The operation is determined by the request body parameters.

CLI Usage:
- To stop: vastai stop instance <ID>
- To start: vastai start instance <ID>
- To label: vastai label instance <ID> <label>

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to destroy

items.docs:
Destroys/deletes an instance permanently. This is irreversible and will delete all data.

CLI Usage: vast-ai destroy instance <id>
Example: vast-ai destroy instance 4242

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to detach the SSH key from

params.name: key
params.description: Numeric ID of the SSH key to detach

items.docs:
Detaches an SSH key from a specified instance, removing SSH access for
that key.


CLI Usage: vastai detach <instance_id> <ssh_key_id>

Example: vastai detach 99999 12345

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to execute command on

items.docs:
Executes a constrained remote command on a specified instance.
The command output can be retrieved from the returned result URL.

CLI Usage: vastai execute <instance_id> <command>
Example: vastai execute 12345 "ls -l"

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to get logs from

items.docs:
Request logs from a specific instance. The logs will be uploaded to S3 and
can be retrieved from a generated URL. Supports both container logs and daemon
system logs.

CLI Usage: vast logs INSTANCE_ID [--tail LINES] [--filter GREP] [--daemon-logs]

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to prepay for

items.docs:
Deposit credits into a reserved instance to receive usage discounts.
The discount rate is calculated based on how many months of usage the prepaid amount covers.
Maximum discount is typically 40%.

CLI Usage: vastai prepay instance <ID> <AMOUNT>
Example: vastai prepay instance 1234 500.0

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to reboot

items.docs:
Stops and starts a container without losing GPU priority. Updates container status to 'rebooting'
and executes docker stop/start commands on the host machine.

CLI Usage: vastai reboot instance <ID>

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the instance to recycle

items.docs:
Destroys and recreates container in place (from newly pulled image) without losing GPU priority.
Updates container status to 'recycling' and executes docker stop/remove commands on the host machine.

CLI Usage: vastai recycle instance <ID>

params.name: instance_id
params.description: The ID of the instance to retrieve SSH keys for.

items.docs: Retrieves the SSH keys associated with a specific instance.

Retrieve a list of instances for the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vast-ai show instances [OPTIONS] [--api-key API_KEY] [--raw]

params.name: machine_id
params.description: ID of the machine to cancel maintenance for.

items.docs: Cancel a scheduled maintenance window for a specified machine.

params.name: machine_id
params.description: The ID of the machine to clean up.

items.docs:
This endpoint removes expired contracts on a specified machine, freeing up space.

CLI Usage: vast-ai cleanup machine {machine_id}

Creates or updates ask contracts for a machine to list it for rent on the vast.ai platform.
Allows setting pricing, minimum GPU requirements, end date and discount rates.

CLI Usage: vast list machine <machine_id> [options]

params.name: machine_id
params.description: ID of the machine to remove the default job from.

items.docs:
Deletes the default job (background instances) for a specified machine.

CLI Usage: vast-ai remove defjob {machine_id}

params.name: machine_id
params.description: The unique identifier of the machine.

items.docs:
Retrieves a list of the most recent reports for a given machine. Each report includes details such as the problem identified, a message describing the issue, and the timestamp when the report was created.

CLI Usage: vastai reports <machine_id>

params.name: machine_id
params.description: ID of the machine to schedule maintenance for.

items.docs:
Schedules a maintenance window for a specified machine and notifies clients.

CLI Usage: vast-ai schedule maint {machine_id} --sdate {sdate} --duration {duration}

Creates default jobs (background instances) for a specified machine with the given parameters.

CLI Usage: vast set defjob {machine_id} --price_gpu {price} --price_inetu {price} --price_inetd {price} --image {image} [--args {args}]

params.name: machine_id
params.description: The ID of the machine.

items.docs:
Sets the minimum bid price for a specified machine.

CLI Usage: vast set min-bid {machine_id} --price {price}

params.name: user_id
params.description: The ID of the user whose machines are being requested.

items.docs: Fetches data for multiple machines associated with the authenticated user.

params.name: machine_id
params.description: The ID of the machine to unlist.

items.docs:
Removes all 'ask' type offer contracts for a specified machine, effectively unlisting it from being available for rent.

CLI Usage: vastai unlist machine <id>

Retrieves all API keys associated with the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vastai show api-keys

Creates a new API key with specified permissions for the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vast create api-key --name NAME --permission_file PERMISSIONS [--key_params PARAMS]

Example:
vast create api-key --name "read-only" --permission_file permissions.json

Retrieve a list of environment variables (secrets) for the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vast-ai show env-vars [-s]

Creates a new encrypted environment variable for the authenticated user.
Keys are automatically converted to uppercase. Values are encrypted before storage.
There is a limit on the total number of environment variables per user.

CLI Usage: vast create env-var KEY VALUE

Updates the value of an existing environment variable for the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vast-ai set env-var KEY VALUE

Deletes an environment variable associated with the authenticated user.
The variable must exist and belong to the requesting user.

CLI Usage: vastai delete env-var <name>
Retrieve a list of SSH keys associated with the authenticated user's account.

Creates a new SSH key and associates it with your account.
The key will be automatically added to all your current instances.

CLI Usage: vast create ssh-key <ssh_key>
Example: vast create ssh-key "ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1..."

Creates either a standalone user account or a subaccount under a parent account.
Subaccounts can be restricted to host-only functionality.

CLI Usage: vastai create subaccount --email EMAIL --username USERNAME --password PASSWORD --type host

Updates the user data for the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vast set user --file {file_path}

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the API key to retrieve

items.docs:
Retrieves an existing API key belonging to the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vastai show api-key ID

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the API key to delete

items.docs:
Deletes an existing API key belonging to the authenticated user.
The API key is soft-deleted by setting a deleted_at timestamp.

CLI Usage: vastai delete api-key ID

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the SSH key to update

items.docs:
Updates the specified SSH key with the provided value.

CLI Usage: vastai update ssh-key id ssh_key

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the SSH key to delete

items.docs:
Removes an SSH key from the authenticated user's account
CLI Usage: vastai delete ssh-key <id>`

Resets the API key for the authenticated user. This operation generates a new API key and invalidates the old one.

CLI Usage: vast-ai reset api-key

Retrieves the list of cloud connections associated with the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vastai show connections

params.name: user_id
params.description: The ID of the user whose IP address history is being retrieved.

items.docs:
This endpoint retrieves the history of IP address accesses for the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vast-ai show ipaddrs

Retrieve a list of subaccounts associated with the authenticated user's account.

CLI Usage: vast-ai show subaccounts

params.name: id
params.description: Name of the team role

items.docs:
Retrieve details of a specific team role by its name.

CLI Usage: vastai show team-role NAME

Retrieve information about the current authenticated user, excluding the API key.

CLI Usage: vastai show user

Transfers specified amount of credits from the authenticated user's account to another user's account.
The recipient can be specified by either email address or user ID.

CLI Usage: vast.py transfer credit <recipient_email> <amount>

Retrieves the list of workergroups associated with the authenticated user.

CLI Usage: vastai show workergroups

Creates a new workergroup configuration that manages worker instances for a serverless endpoint.

CLI Usage: vast-ai create workergroup --template_hash <HASH> --endpoint_name <NAME> [options]
Retrieve a list of endpoint jobs for the authenticated user.

This endpoint creates a new job processing endpoint with specified parameters.

CLI Usage: vast-ai create endpoint

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the workergroup to update.

items.docs:
Updates the properties of an existing workergroup based on the provided parameters.

CLI Usage: vastai update workergroup <ID> [OPTIONS]

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the workergroup to delete

items.docs:
Deletes an existing workergroup.

CLI Usage: vastai delete workergroup <ID>

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the endpoint group to update

items.docs:
Updates the specified endpoint group with the provided parameters.

CLI Usage: vastai update endpoint ID [OPTIONS]

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the endpoint group to delete

items.docs:
Deletes an endpoint group by ID. Associated workergroups will also be deleted.

CLI Usage: vastai delete endpoint <ID>
Retrieves logs for a specific workergroup by ID.

Retrieves the current list and status of workers for a specific workergroup.

Useful for monitoring, debugging connectivity issues, and understanding resource usage within a workergroup.
Retrieves logs for a specific endpoint by name.

Retrieves the current list and status of workers for a specific endpoint.

Useful for monitoring, debugging connectivity issues, and understanding resource usage.

Calls on the serverless engine to retrieve a GPU instance address within your endpoint for processing a request.

The engine will return either a ready worker URL or status information if no workers are available.

Creates a new team with the authenticated user as the owner. The user's existing API key
becomes the owner key for the team. The user cannot already be a team or be a member of another team.

The operation:
1. Converts the user into a team
2. Creates default team roles (member, manager, owner)
3. Converts user's API key into the owner key
4. Adds the creating user as a team member

CLI Usage: vast create team --name <team_name>

Creates a new role within a team. Only team owners or managers with the appropriate permissions can perform this operation.

CLI Usage: vast create team role --name <role_name> --permissions <permissions_json>

Deletes a team and all associated data including API keys, rights, invitations,
memberships and metadata. The team owner's master API key is converted to a normal client key.

CLI Usage: vast-ai destroy team

Sends an invitation email to the specified user to join the team with the given role.

CLI Usage: vast-ai invite team-member --email EMAIL --role ROLE

params.name: id
params.description: User ID of the team member to remove

items.docs:
Removes a member from the team by revoking their team-related API keys and updating membership status.
Cannot remove the team owner.

CLI Usage: vast-ai remove team-member ID
Example: vast-ai remove team-member 12345

params.name: id
params.description: Name of the role to remove

items.docs:
Removes a role from the team. Cannot remove the team owner role.

CLI Usage: vast-ai remove team-role NAME
Example: vast-ai remove team-role developer

Retrieve a list of team members associated with the authenticated user's team.

CLI Usage: vastai show team-members

params.name: id
params.description: ID of the role to update.

items.docs: Update an existing team role with new name and permissions.

Retrieve a list of all roles for a team, excluding the 'team_owner' role.

CLI Usage: vastai show team-roles

params.name: query
params.description: Search query string to match against template fields.

params.name: select_filters
params.description: Filters to apply on the search results.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Column to order the results by.

items.docs:
Searches for templates based on query parameters and retrieves matching templates.

CLI Usage: vast search templates

params.name: query
params.description: Search query string to filter benchmarks.

items.docs:
Retrieve benchmark data based on search parameters.

CLI Usage: vast-ai search benchmarks

Search for available GPU machine offers with advanced filtering and sorting.
Default filters applied unless --no-default: - verified: true - external: false - rentable: true - rented: false
CLI Usage Examples: ``` vast search offers 'reliability > 0.99 num_gpus>=4' vast search offers 'gpu_name=RTX_3090' --order=dph_total vast search offers 'cuda_vers>=11.0' --no-default ```

Creates a new template for launching instances. If an identical template already exists,
returns the existing template instead of creating a duplicate.

CLI Usage: vast create template [options]
Delete a volume by its ID. CLI Usage: vastai delete volume VOLUME_ID
Retrieve information about all volumes rented by you. CLI Usage: vastai show volumes
Rent/create a new volume with specified parameters. CLI Usage: vastai create volume ID --size SIZE_GB

params.name: q
params.description: Query parameters for filtering volume search results

items.docs: Search for available volumes based on specified criteria. CLI Usage: vastai search volumes <query> [OPTIONS]
Remove a volume listing from the marketplace. CLI Usage: vastai unlist volume VOLUME_ID

params.name: select_filters
params.description: Filters to apply to the invoice selection.

params.name: type
params.description: Type of invoices to retrieve (e.g., charge).

items.docs:
This endpoint allows users to search and retrieve invoices based on specified filters.

CLI Usage: vast-ai search invoices

params.name: id
params.description: The ID of the instance.

items.docs:
Retrieves the deposit details for a specified instance.

CLI Usage: vast show deposit {ID}

params.name: user_id
params.description: The ID of the user.

params.name: sday
params.description: Start day for the earnings report.

params.name: eday
params.description: End day for the earnings report.

params.name: machid
params.description: Optional machine ID to filter earnings.

params.name: last_days
params.description: Number of days to look back from today.

items.docs:
Retrieves the earnings history for a specified time range and optionally per machine.

CLI Usage: vastai show earnings [OPTIONS]

params.name: user_id
params.description: The ID of the user whose invoices are being retrieved.

params.name: inc_charges
params.description: Include charge items in the report.

params.name: only_payouts
params.description: Show only payout items.

params.name: no_payouts
params.description: Exclude payout items.

params.name: sdate
params.description: Start date and time for the report.

params.name: edate
params.description: End date and time for the report.

items.docs:
This endpoint retrieves billing history reports for the authenticated user, including charges and credits.

CLI Usage: vast-ai show invoices
